tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Arabic language world (Contemporary Arab Town)
tasks.description: Create a complete Prolog world dataset at data/worlds/language/arabic/ for an Arabic language-learning world set in a contemporary Arab coastal town — a sun-warmed medina with whitewashed walls alongside modern cafes, a bustling university district, traditional souks selling spices and electronics, and a corniche promenade. Model EVERY file after data/worlds/language/louisiana_french/ as the reference implementation. Read each louisiana_french .pl file first to match exact predicate formats. Create ALL of the following files: (1) world.pl — world/2, world_description/2, world_type (modern_realistic), game_type (language_learning), target_language (arabic), country/3, state/3, and all metadata predicates. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 settlements with settlement/3, settlement_description/2, settlement_type/2, settlement_population/2. (3) locations.pl — at least 30 lots with lot/3, lot_type/2, lot_district/2, lot_street/2, buildings via building/3, at least 10 businesses via business/3 (cafes, souks, university buildings, mosques, bakeries, pharmacies, etc.). (4) characters.pl — at least 20 characters with person/1, culturally authentic Arabic names, family trees across generations, location assignments. (5) quests.pl — at least 15 quests spanning CEFR A1-B2, covering conversation, exploration, vocabulary, grammar, cultural_knowledge types with quest objectives and prerequisite chains. (6) items.pl — at least 20 culturally relevant items (Arabic coffee sets, calligraphy pens, smartphones, textbooks, spices, traditional clothing, etc.). (7) languages.pl — Arabic language definition with language_family, script, speakers — specifying Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) as the learning target. (8) grammars.pl — at least 3 Tracery grammars including authentic Arabic name generation via grammar/2, grammar_rule/3. (9) truths.pl — at least 20 world truths covering cultural norms, social rules, linguistic notes about Arabic (formality, diglossia, right-to-left). (10) history.pl — at least 15 historical events for the world timeline. IMPORTANT: Never use apostrophes or single quotes in Prolog comments — they break syntax highlighting. Use double-quoted alternatives or rephrase. All files must be valid Prolog with snake_case atoms and single-quoted display strings.

tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Bengali language world (Contemporary Bangladesh)

Create ALL of the following files: (1) world.pl — world/2, world_description/2, world_type (modern_realistic), game_type (language_learning), target_language (bengali), country/3, state/3, and all metadata predicates. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 settlements with culturally authentic names. (3) locations.pl — at least 30 lots with buildings and at least 10 businesses (tea stalls, textile shops, pharmacies, bookstores, fish markets, sweet shops, etc.). (4) characters.pl — at least 20 characters with authentic Bengali names, family trees, location assignments. (5) quests.pl — at least 15 quests spanning CEFR A1-B2. (9) truths.pl — at least 20 world truths about Bengali culture, social customs, and linguistic features. (10) history.pl — at least 15 historical events. IMPORTANT: Never use apostrophes or single quotes in Prolog comments — they break syntax highlighting. Use double-quoted alternatives or rephrase. All files must be valid Prolog.

tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Breton language world (Modern Independent Brittany)
tasks.description: Create a complete Prolog world dataset at data/worlds/language/breton/ for a Breton language-learning world set in a modern independent Republic of Brittany (Republik Breizh) — an alternate-history nation where Brittany achieved sovereignty. A coastal town with a working fishing harbor, creperies and cider bars, Breton-language schools (Diwan schools), fest-noz music venues, Celtic cultural centers, standing stone heritage sites, and a bilingual Breton-French street life. Model EVERY file after data/worlds/language/louisiana_french/ as the reference implementation (Louisiana French uses a similar alternate-nation concept with Royaume de Louisiane). Read each louisiana_french .pl file first to match exact predicate formats. Create ALL files: (1) world.pl — world_type (alternate_modern), game_type (language_learning), target_language (breton), country (Republic of Brittany / Republik Breizh), state, metadata. (2) settlements.pl — at least 2 Breton settlements. (3) locations.pl — at least 30 lots with buildings and 10+ businesses (creperies, fishing co-ops, Diwan schools, Celtic music shops, cider presses, bookstores, surf shops, etc.). (4) characters.pl — at least 20 characters with authentic Breton names, family trees. (5) quests.pl — at least 15 CEFR A1-B2 quests. (6) items.pl — at least 20 items (crepes, cider, Celtic knot jewelry, Breton flags, surfboards, smartphones, Breton-language books, coiffes for festivals, etc.). (7) languages.pl — Breton language definition (Celtic family) specifying modern Breton as the learning target. (8) grammars.pl — at least 3 Tracery grammars with Breton name generation. (9) truths.pl — at least 20 truths about Breton culture, language mutations, maritime customs, modern Celtic identity. (10) history.pl — at least 15 historical events including the alternate-history independence. IMPORTANT: Never use apostrophes or single quotes in Prolog comments — they break syntax highlighting. Use double-quoted alternatives or rephrase. All files must be valid Prolog.

tasks.title: Generate complete Prolog dataset for Creole Colonial world
